question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamp values that are just above Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 (i.e., Long.MAX_VALUE itself), causing findByTimestamp() to always return empty (no record file has consensusEnd >= Long.MAX_VALUE), bypassing the cache (null not cached), and by repeating this at high frequency, exhaust database connections and cause non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block type strings that are very long (e.g., thousands of characters) before the switch statement in BlockType.of() calls value.toLowerCase(), causing excessive memory allocation for the lowercase string conversion and string comparison operations, and by sending many such requests concurrently, exhaust JVM heap memory and cause non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block type strings that are very long hex values (e.g., '0x' followed by thousands of characters), causing extractNumericBlock() to call Strings.CS.removeStart() and Long.parseLong() on a very long string, throwing an IllegalArgumentException that includes the full string in the message `'Invalid block value: ' + value`, causing large string allocations in exception objects and exhausting JVM heap memory under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block type strings containing only the '0x' prefix and no digits (e.g., '0x'), causing extractNumericBlock() to call Long.parseLong('', 16) which throws a NumberFormatException, and by sending thousands of such requests, cause excessive exception object creation and garbage collection pressure, resulting in non-network-based DoS through GC exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamps that are valid but correspond to record files with very large byte[] fields (bytes, logsBloom, previousWrappedRecordBlockHash, wrappedRecordBlockHash), causing findByTimestamp() to load and cache these large objects in both CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_TIMESTAMP and CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX, exhausting JVM heap memory and causing non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block numbers that are valid but correspond to record files with large `items` collections (List<RecordItem>), causing findByIndex() to load and cache these large collections in CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX, exhausting JVM heap memory and causing non-network-based DoS through memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input validation on the timestamp parameter in findByTimestamp(Long timestamp) by sending requests with timestamps that are 0 or negative, causing the DB query to return the earliest record file on every call, and if the cache key for timestamp=0 is not pre-populated, trigger repeated DB queries causing non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block numbers that are valid long values but exceed the actual number of record files in the database (e.g., index = 999999999999), causing findByIndex() to always return empty Optional (null not cached), triggering a DB query on every request, and by sending these at high frequency, exhaust database connection pool and cause non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamps that are incrementally increasing by 1 nanosecond, causing each request to generate a unique cache key in CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_TIMESTAMP, filling the cache with duplicate RecordFile objects (same file, different timestamp keys), exhausting JVM heap memory and causing non-network-based DoS through cache memory exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block type 'safe', 'pending', or 'finalized' strings that all resolve to BlockType.LATEST singleton, and if the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_LATEST cache is shared and has limited capacity, cause cache eviction of other entries by flooding with LATEST requests, degrading performance for other block type lookups and causing non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamps that are exactly equal to the consensusEnd of the latest record file, causing findByTimestamp() to return that record file and write it to CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX via @CachePut, and by repeating this with slightly different timestamps that all map to the same record file, cause excessive cache write operations that degrade cache performance and cause non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block numbers that are valid hex strings with leading zeros (e.g., '0x0000000000000001'), causing extractNumericBlock() to parse these correctly but create new BlockType objects with the original string as the name, and if these are used as cache keys, cause cache fragmentation where the same block index is cached under multiple keys, wasting memory and causing non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: findByTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamps that are just below the consensusEnd of the latest record file, causing findByTimestamp() to return the latest record file and write it to CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX, and by sending many such requests with slightly different timestamps, cause the index cache to be repeatedly written with the same entry, consuming cache write resources and causing non-network-based DoS?
